allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops help red-queen-effect turn constant environmental pressure into iterative learning instead of blind acceleration.
First-principles-thinking helps red-queen-effect escape stale incrementalism when conventional responses no longer keep pace with a changing baseline.
allies.source_quote: First Principles strips away the assumptions associated with conventional methods, allowing the thinker to forge a truly novel, non-obvious solution.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks red-queen-effect by making teams defend the old model instead of adapting to disconfirming competitive evidence.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation bias is the instinct to process information to get a conclusion we want rather than what is true, leading to model-defending behavior.

antagonists.rationale_text: Margin-of-safety resists red-queen-effect when speed pressure would otherwise push adaptation below acceptable downside buffers.
antagonists.source_quote: Red Queen Effect conflicts with Margin Of Safety when speed pressure and continuous competitive response reduce downside buffers below acceptable thresholds.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Red-queen-effect conflicts with margin-of-safety when speed pressure and continuous competitive response reduce downside buffers below acceptable thresholds.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Red Queen Effect conflicts with Margin Of Safety when speed pressure and continuous competitive response reduce downside buffers below acceptable thresholds.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on feedback-guided adaptation, anti-entrenchment, and one clean current-vocabulary tension with safety-buffer discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor rivalry relation sprawl beyond the clean feedback, anti-confirmation, and margin-of-safety surface.
open_questions: The source also supports an outside-view relation, but the retained set favors the stronger current-vocabulary adaptation and guardrail targets.
